title: Guide 0104 – Home Child Care Provider and Home Support Worker - Canada.ca
content:
## Guide 0104 – Home Child Care Provider and Home Support Worker
We’re no longer accepting new applications
The pilots ended on June 17, 2024. We’ll continue to process applications we received on or before this date.
Learn more about the new Home Care Worker Immigration pilots and how to apply.
New changes to the pilots
Updated June 24, 2024
These changes are now in effect and apply to all applications being processed:
- You only need 6 months of work experience, instead of 12, to qualify.
- For the Gaining experience category, we’ll accept eligible work experience from inside or outside Canada gained anytime between the 36-month period before you submitted your permanent residence application and the date you provide proof of work experience
- from inside or outside Canada
- gained anytime between the 36-month period before you submitted your permanent residence application and the date you provide proof of work experience
If you already applied to the Gaining experience category and now have enough eligible work experience, you can send us proof .
Under the Home Child Care Provider Pilot and Home Support Worker Pilot, if you have less than 12 months of eligible Canadian work experience at the time you apply, you will be assessed for permanent residence eligibility before you begin working in Canada. Once you have acquired 12 months of eligible work experience under an occupation-restricted work permit (within a 36 months period after the work permit was issued), you will have access to a direct pathway to become a permanent resident. This is called the Gaining experience category.
If you have already acquired at least 12 months of eligible work experience in Canada as a home child care provider or home support worker, within the last 36 months at the time of applying, you can apply for permanent residence without needing to acquire further work experience under these pilots. This is called the Direct to permanent residence (PR) category.

## Do you intend to work in Quebec?
If you intend to reside in the province of Quebec , you do not qualify to apply under these pilots.
To be eligible for these pilots, you must show that you intend to reside as a permanent resident in a province other than Quebec.
If you have less than 12 months of eligible Canadian work experience at the time of your application for permanent residence, you must have a job offer for a position in a province outside Quebec.
If you have at least 12 months of eligible work experience, and some or all of this experience was gained in Quebec, you can still use it to apply under these pilots if you currently live outside of Quebec or you plan to move to another province or territory. Find out more .

## Tell us about your experience
There are different application forms and instructions for these pilots depending on:
- where you’re submitting your application from, and
- which category you’re applying under (Gaining experience or Direct to PR)

### Answer the following questions to get the forms and instructions for your situation

## Which of the following options best describes your situation?
Option 1: You have already acquired at least 12 months of qualifying work experience in Canada in the last 36 months (at the time of application) under NOC 4411 or NOC  4412
Option 2: You have less than 12 months of work experience and are applying from outside of Canada
Option 3: You have less than 12 months of work experience, are applying from within Canada, and are eligible to apply for a work permit from within Canada .
Option 4: You have less than 12 months of work experience, are applying from within Canada, but are not eligible to apply for a work permit from within Canada .
You are eligible to apply from within Canada if you meet any of the following criteria:
- you have a valid study or work permit, have maintained your status or are eligible for restoration
- your spouse, common-law partner or parents have a valid study or  work permit
- you’ve graduated from a program at a Canadian: university community college CÉGEP publicly funded trade/technical school, or other eligible school
- publicly funded trade/technical school, or
- other eligible school
- you’re waiting on a decision on a separate application for permanent residence from inside Canada
You can apply under the Direct to permanent residence category. The instructions are in the following guide.
Continue to the guide 0104 B
You can apply under the Gaining experience category. Even if you are in Canada, you will include a work permit application for outside Canada applicants. The instructions are in the following guide.
Continue to the guide 0104 A1
You can apply under the Gaining experience category. You will include a work permit application from outside Canada. The instructions are in the following guide.
Continue to the guide 0104 A1
When you have obtained at least 12 months of Canadian experience as a home child care provider or home support worker, you may return to this instructions guide to apply under the Direct to permanent residence category. Note that the pilots will expire on June 17, 2024.
You can apply under the Gaining experience category. You will include a work permit application for in-Canada applicants. The instructions are found in the following guide.
Continue to the guide 0104 A2

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
